description: The numeral representing doubled wholeness, the expansion of the four-fold cycle. More than simple doubling, 'rotu' acknowledges how certain patterns repeat at higher levels—the way eight creates a fuller cycle while maintaining the stability of four.
sound_symbolism: The word begins with 'r', the rolling liquid that suggests repetition and return. This meets 'o', the round vowel of cycles and wholeness. The transition through 't', the clear stop, to 'u', the deep back vowel, creates a journey from rolling cycles to grounded depth. The progression from cyclic flow ('ro') to deep stability ('tu') mirrors how eight operates—expanded repetition with profound stability.
grammatical_notes: Functions as the eighth cardinal numeral. Requires the plural particle 'lo' when modifying nouns. Often conceptualized in relation to four (double four) or as a significant point in counting systems. May appear in contexts involving extended cycles, musical octaves, or expanded organizational systems.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Eight holds special significance as the Noble Eightfold Path—right understanding, thought, speech, action, livelihood, effort, mindfulness, concentration. 'Rotu' embodies this complete system for ending suffering.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Traditional systems recognize eight as double-four, expanding basic cycles—eight phases of the moon, eight seasonal markers, eight directions (cardinal plus intercardinal). 'Rotu' carries this understanding of cycles within cycles.
